Ordinary passport holders (including Hong Kong SAR and Macao SAR) may enter and stay in Georgia without visa for 30 days for tourism purposes only. Based on PM Ordinance No. 359 (11/09/2023) and formalized via bilateral visa exemption agreement signed 10 April 2024, in force 28 May 2024. Non-tourism purposes (work, study, business requiring authorization) still require a visa.

Citizens of the Hashemite Kingdom of Jordan may enter and stay in Georgia without a visa for one full year, as confirmed by the Georgian Embassy in Jordan. Study purposes require a separate D3 Study Visa.

Citizens may enter and stay in Georgia without visa for 90 days within a 180-day period. Added to the visa-free list as of April 1, 2024 (Ordinance No. 147, April 23, 2024). Previously in the simplified e-visa category.

e_visa.notes: Standard e-visa category. Requires: biometric photo, passport copy, accommodation proof, flight ticket, travel insurance, financial means or travel purpose proof. Processing: not less than 5 working days. Fee: USD 20 + 2% service fee. Stay duration depends on passport country (typically 30 days per entry within 120-day validity, or 90 days within 180-day validity).
Standard e-visa category. Requires: biometric photo, passport copy, accommodation proof, flight ticket, travel insurance, financial means or travel purpose proof.

visa_policy.notes: Georgia's visa-free list is governed by Government Ordinance No. 255 (05/06/2015, as amended). As of February 2026 (Ordinance No. 80, 24/02/2026), approximately 98 countries' citizens may enter and stay visa-free. The core 94-country list grants 365-day stays. Additions since 2023 include: China (ordinary passport, 30 days, tourism only, since 11/09/2023 per PM Ordinance No. 359; formalized by bilateral agreement in force 28/05/2024); Jordan (365 days); Peru (90 days in 180-day period, since Ordinance No. 147, 23/04/2024). EU citizens may use identity cards. UN Laissez-Passer holders receive equivalent privileges. A separate ordinance (2867377) allows citizens of 50 specific countries to enter visa-free for 90 days in any 180-day period if they hold a valid visa or residence permit from qualifying OECD/Schengen countries. Effective 17 April 2025 (Ordinance No. 122), 17 nationalities under the second ordinance now require a multiple-entry visa or permit valid for at least 1 year at entry. E-visa fee: USD 20 + 2% service fee; processing: not less than 5 working days; stay duration varies by nationality (typically 30 days per visit within 120-day validity, or 90 days within 180-day validity).

outbound_official.notes: The official Georgian MFA consular portal (geoconsul.gov.ge) has a dedicated section 'Information on visa-free travel to EU / Schengen member states' for Georgian citizens. This page confirms Georgian biometric passport holders may enter the 26 Schengen Area countries (22 EU members + Iceland, Liechtenstein, Norway, Switzerland) visa-free for up to 90 days in any 180-day period, in force since 28 March 2017. The page is a JavaScript SPA and was not directly scrapable; content confirmed via search-engine snippets and official EU/Georgia sources. Georgia does NOT publish a comprehensive government-maintained outbound list covering all destinations (no equivalent of India MEA VFFIN); for non-Schengen destinations Georgian citizens must rely on destination-country rules. Bulgaria, Cyprus, and Romania are EU members but not Schengen; Ireland (EU) requires a separate visa. ETIAS (European Travel Information and Authorisation System) is expected to apply to Georgian citizens for Schengen travel from 2026 onward.

cbi.program_name: Georgian Citizenship by Exception / Special Contribution Citizenship Program

options.notes: Investment in a Georgian business employing at least 10 local citizens. Based on Article 17(2)(b) of the Organic Law of Georgia on Georgian Citizenship (investment contributing substantially to state economy development).
Refundable contribution to a government-approved fund, held for at least 5 years then fully refunded. Announced February 2025 as part of the Special Contribution Citizenship Program.

Non-refundable contribution to a special state fund. A discounted early-applicant rate of EUR 250,000 was offered at program launch (February 2025). Based on President's authority under Article 17(1) for exceptional merit/contribution.

cbi.notes: Georgia does not have a standalone CBI program in the traditional sense. Citizenship by exception is granted by the President under Article 17 of the Organic Law on Georgian Citizenship. The 'Special Contribution Citizenship Program' was announced February 24, 2025 by Deputy Foreign Minister Khvtisiashvili. Dual citizenship is permitted for those granted citizenship under Article 17 and Article 21-1 of the law. No language test or residency required for citizenship by exception. Regular naturalization requires 10 years of continuous lawful residence. The specific investment amounts (EUR 3M business / EUR 2M refundable fund / EUR 300K donation) were announced for the program but the official government implementing regulation details were not directly verifiable on matsne.gov.ge at time of research — figures sourced from government official announcements reported in February 2025.

programs.name: Investment Residence Permit (Real Estate)
programs.notes: Requires ownership of immovable property (excluding agricultural land) with a certified market value of at least USD 300,000 equivalent in GEL. Property valuation by accredited assessor required. Effective March 1, 2026, the threshold was increased from USD 100,000. Permit issued for 5 years, renewable. Cumulative validity not to exceed 12 years. After 5 years may convert to Residence Permit for Indefinite Stay. Path to citizenship requires 10 years of continuous residence with language/history test. Family members (spouse, minor children, dependents) may be included. Based on the Law of Georgia on the Legal Status of Aliens and Stateless Persons, Article 15(h).

programs.name: Investment Residence Permit (Business Investment)
programs.notes: Requires at least USD 300,000 equivalent in GEL invested in business activities in Georgia (verified by audit report). Annual turnover thresholds apply: USD 50,000 (year 1), USD 100,000 (year 2), USD 120,000 (years 3-5). The Procedures for Reviewing Residence Permit Applications (matsne.gov.ge/en/document/view/2483468) states a minimum investment of GEL 300,000 (approximately USD 100,000-110,000 depending on exchange rate) for the application. Applications processed within 30 days. Written recommendation from a government member or three Georgian business representatives required. Permit duration up to 6 years.

programs.name: Remotely from Georgia
programs.eligibility: Citizens of visa-free countries (98+ nationalities) who are remote workers, freelancers, full-time employees, or business owners working for foreign employers. Minimum income USD 2,000/month or USD 24,000 in savings required. Health insurance required.
programs.notes: Program was launched in 2020 during COVID-19. Allows visa-free nationals to formalize a 360-day stay in Georgia as a remote worker. As of June 2025, Georgia passed amendments requiring mandatory work permits for foreign nationals (including remote workers) starting March 1, 2026, which may affect the program's status. Official program page appears to have been removed from active government portals; geoconsul.gov.ge/en/news/16 referenced but content not directly accessible. Current status uncertain as of June 2026.

conditional_access.conditions: Alien holding a valid visa and/or residence permit of any country listed in Government Ordinance No. 256 (5 June 2015, as amended) — Australia, Austria, USA, UAE, New Zealand, Bahrain, Belgium, Bulgaria, Germany, Denmark (incl. Faroe Islands and Greenland), UK (incl. overseas territories and Crown Dependencies), Spain, Estonia, Japan, Ireland, Iceland, Israel, Italy, Canada, Qatar, Cyprus, South Korea, Latvia, Lithuania, Liechtenstein, Luxembourg, Malta, Netherlands (incl. Aruba and Netherlands Antilles), Norway, Oman, Poland, Portugal, Romania, Greece, France (incl. French Polynesia and New Caledonia), Saudi Arabia, Slovakia, Slovenia, Hungary, Finland, Kuwait, Sweden, Switzerland, Czech Republic, Croatia — may enter and stay in Georgia without visa for 90 calendar days in any 180-day period. Visa/permit must be valid on the day of entry (crossing the border). Effective 17 April 2025 (Ordinance No. 122), nationals of Afghanistan, Bangladesh, Cameroon, DRC, Côte d'Ivoire, Eritrea, Ethiopia, Ghana, Morocco, Nigeria, Pakistan, Somalia, Sudan, Syria, Tanzania, Uganda, and Yemen must hold a multiple-entry visa or residence permit valid for not less than 1 year at the time of entry.

Alien holding a valid residence permit of any country listed in Government Ordinance No. 256 (5 June 2015, as amended) — same 50-country list as for visa holders — may enter and stay in Georgia without visa for 90 calendar days in any 180-day period. Permit must be valid on the day of entry (crossing the border). Effective 17 April 2025 (Ordinance No. 122), nationals of Afghanistan, Bangladesh, Cameroon, DRC, Côte d'Ivoire, Eritrea, Ethiopia, Ghana, Morocco, Nigeria, Pakistan, Somalia, Sudan, Syria, Tanzania, Uganda, and Yemen must hold a multiple-entry residence permit valid for not less than 1 year at the time of entry.

Holders of valid diplomatic passports, service passports, and passports for public affairs (official passports) issued by the People's Republic of China (including Hong Kong and Macao SAR) may enter and stay in Georgia without a visa for 30 days. Based on bilateral visa exemption agreement between Georgia and China in force since 3 February 1994 (as referenced in China NIA mutual visa exemption records). Ordinary passport holders from China are separately covered by the 11 September 2023 ordinance and the 28 May 2024 bilateral agreement (30 days, tourism only) and are listed in visa_free.

Holders of valid diplomatic and official/service passports issued by Pakistan may enter, transit through, leave, and stay in Georgia without a visa for up to 90 days within any 180-day period. Based on 'Agreement between the Government of Georgia and the Government of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan on Visa Exemption for Holders of Diplomatic and Official/Service Passports', signed 21 September 2023, entered into force 2 April 2024.

Citizens of India, Nepal, Peru, Indonesia, Egypt, Guyana, and Sri Lanka holding valid diplomatic or official/service passports issued by their country may enter and stay in Georgia without a visa for a specified period under bilateral visa suppression/waiver agreements. Exact stay duration per bilateral agreement not specified on the source page. Confirmed via official Georgian tourism authority source (georgia.travel).

validation_notes: CORRECTION: China (CHN) moved from e_visa to visa_free. Ordinary Chinese passport holders (incl. HK and Macao SAR) have been visa-free since PM Ordinance No. 359 (11/09/2023, 30 days, tourism only), formalized by bilateral agreement in force 28/05/2024. Source: china.mfa.gov.ge/en/important-information and mfa.gov.ge.

CORRECTION: Jordan (JOR) moved from e_visa to visa_free. Jordanian citizens may enter and stay in Georgia without a visa for one full year (365 days) as confirmed by the Georgian Embassy to Jordan official page. Source: jordan.mfa.gov.ge/en/visa-information.

CORRECTION: Peru (PER) moved from e_visa (simplified category) to visa_free with max_stay_days set to 90. Peru was added to the visa-free list as of April 1, 2024 (Ordinance No. 147, 23/04/2024). Duration is 90 days within a 180-day period. Source: matsne.gov.ge/en/document/view/2867361.
CORRECTION: Jordan entry removed from e_visa list (was incorrectly listed there in prior record).
CORRECTION: China entry removed from e_visa list (was incorrectly listed there in prior record).
CORRECTION: Peru entry removed from e_visa simplified category list (moved to visa_free).

UPDATED: visa_policy notes and fast_track eligibility updated to reflect approximately 98 visa-free countries (up from 94) following amendments including Ordinance No. 80 (24/02/2026) and Ordinance No. 147 (23/04/2024).

NOTED: Gabon appears in both the conditional (Category 1) and standard (Category 2) e-visa listings on the live evisa.gov.ge portal — both pathways appear available; conditional_access entry updated with clarifying note.

VERIFIED: The 17-country restriction list in conditional_access (requiring multiple-entry visa/permit valid 1+ year as of April 17, 2025) confirmed via UAE Embassy of Georgia page and passportindex.org citing official Georgian government sources: Afghanistan, Bangladesh, Cameroon, DRC, Cote d'Ivoire, Eritrea, Ethiopia, Ghana, Morocco, Nigeria, Pakistan, Somalia, Sudan, Syria, Tanzania, Uganda, Yemen.
VERIFIED: conditional_access Ordinance No. 256 credential pathway (90 days/180) confirmed unchanged.
VERIFIED: CBI program details (EUR 3M business / EUR 2M fund / EUR 300K donation) not yet confirmed via official matsne.gov.ge implementing regulation — retained with existing caveat notes.
VERIFIED: RBI real estate threshold increased to USD 300,000 effective March 1, 2026 (from USD 100,000) — confirmed as already correctly stated in existing record.
China's ordinary passport note updated to reference both the 2023 ordinance and the 2024 bilateral agreement.
gaps: Exact additional countries added by Ordinance No. 80 (24/02/2026) to the visa-free list beyond China, Jordan, and Peru could not be identified from publicly available sources. The matsne.gov.ge ordinance page references this amendment but specific newly added nationalities were not retrievable from the document portal during this research session.

Exact current text of the April 17, 2025 amendment to Ordinance 2867377 (adding the 17-country multi-entry requirement) could not be directly scraped from matsne.gov.ge — the amendment details were confirmed via official government domain search results and UAE embassy of Georgia page.

E-visa maximum stay duration per nationality is not published as a simple table on evisa.gov.ge — it varies by passport country and requires selecting in the portal; only general ranges (30/120 days or 90/180 days) confirmed from official FAQ.

The Special Contribution Citizenship Program (CBI) investment amounts (EUR 3M business / EUR 2M fund / EUR 300K donation) were announced by a government official in February 2025 but the specific implementing regulation was not located on matsne.gov.ge at time of research.

Syria listed as e-visa eligible based on its inclusion in the second visa ordinance (2867377) update list, but its standalone e-visa eligibility from evisa.gov.ge was not directly confirmed on the countries page.
Georgia has no ETA (electronic travel authority) system.
No visa-on-arrival program found on official sources.
The 'Remotely from Georgia' digital nomad program official page (geoconsul.gov.ge/en/news/16) returned no readable content — program current status uncertain due to March 2026 work permit amendments.
Georgia left the CIS in August 2009 and is not a member of any formal freedom-of-movement area; no regional agreements applicable.

Exact stay durations for the India/Nepal/Peru/Indonesia/Egypt/Guyana/Sri Lanka diplomatic/official/service passport bilateral agreements could not be confirmed from an official Georgian MFA source — georgia.travel confirms eligibility but not stay duration per agreement. The geoconsul.gov.ge detailed page (id=956) was inaccessible during research.
No OCI (Overseas Citizen of India) cardholder-specific conditional entry rule found on any official Georgian government source — not applicable to Georgia.

The full list of all bilateral visa waiver agreements for diplomatic/service/official passports with Georgia was not available as a consolidated document on matsne.gov.ge or mfa.gov.ge at time of research.

Gabon's dual appearance in Category 1 (conditional) and Category 2 (standard) on evisa.gov.ge could not be definitively explained — may be a portal data error or reflect two genuinely separate e-visa pathways.

Peru's exact ordinance number and implementation date for its visa-free status could not be confirmed directly from matsne.gov.ge (Ordinance No. 147, April 23, 2024 referenced but document text not verified).

sources_checked.description: Official Georgian law portal — Ordinance No. 255: List of countries whose citizens may enter Georgia without a visa (visa-free list, 94+ countries, 365-day stay, most recent amendment Ordinance No. 80, 24/02/2026)
Official Georgian law portal — Ordinance: List of countries whose visa/residence permit holders may enter Georgia without a visa (50 qualifying issuers, 90 days in 180)

Official Georgia e-visa portal — countries list with three e-visa categories (Category 1: conditional with Schengen/OECD requirement, 16 countries; Category 2: standard, 53 countries; Category 3: simplified, 29 countries)
Official Georgia e-visa portal FAQ — fees, processing time, stay duration
Organic Law of Georgia on Georgian Citizenship — citizenship by exception, dual citizenship, Article 17
Law of Georgia on the Legal Status of Aliens and Stateless Persons — investment residence permit provisions
Procedures for Reviewing and Deciding the Granting of Georgian Residence Permits — investment residence permit documentation and processing
Georgian MFA geoconsul — Remotely from Georgia program announcement (content not accessible)
Georgian MFA geoconsul — Do I need a visa page (dynamic JS content, limited extraction)
Georgia official tourism authority — visa application page listing diplomatic/official/service passport exempt countries (China, Egypt, Guyana, Indonesia, India, Sri Lanka, Nepal, Peru)
Georgian MFA — Georgia-Pakistan visa waiver agreement announcement (diplomatic and official/service passports, signed 21 Sep 2023, in force 2 Apr 2024, 90 days/180)

Embassy of Georgia to UAE — confirms April 2025 amendment details: 17 restricted nationalities (15 cited in this page, consistent with confirmed 17 total from other sources) must hold multiple-entry visa/permit valid not less than 1 year
China NIA (National Immigration Administration) — mutual visa exemption details confirming Georgia-China diplomatic/service/official passport agreement in force since 3 February 1994, 30-day stay
Embassy of Georgia to China — confirms Chinese citizens (including HK and Macao SAR) may enter Georgia without a visa for 30 days for tourism purposes only, since 11 September 2023
Embassy of Georgia to Jordan — confirms Jordanian citizens may enter and stay in Georgia without a visa for one full year
Georgian MFA — Bilateral Visa Exemption Agreement for ordinary passport holders between Georgia and China, signed April 10, 2024, in force May 28, 2024
